title: Should I watch Rebuild yet
wikitext:
[]: Should I watch Rebuild yet?             Quote Post by Princess Asuka » Tue Oct 22, 2013 4:37 pm I'm debating on whether or not to watch it yet after already having seen the series and End of Evangelion, also local libraries in my area have copies of 1.0, 1.11, and 2.22. I've also heard that 3.33 leaves off on a bad cliffhanger and that I should wait until final comes out before watching rebuild. Any opinions on this are welcome because I kinda wanna watch it, but I kinda wanna wait for Final to be released too.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Oct 22, 2013 4:50 pm The production of the films has been painfully slow for me personally. I would say wait till the last one is out, but only if you really hate to wait for these sorts of things. You could read the manga series in the meantime?

[]:             Quote Post by monitoradiation » Tue Oct 22, 2013 5:11 pm Sure, why not? Moar Eva is good for your mental health! (Enhancing your probability of becoming a hermit and going on EGF notwithstanding)

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Oct 22, 2013 5:29 pm I held off on watching Rebuild for years and didn't watch the first 2 until last year (but still bought some merch) because I was worried that a reboot would be screwed up. However after watching 1.0 and 2.0 due to the upcoming release of 3.0 my fears were unfounded and after watching 3.0 three times, I've fully bought in.

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Tue Oct 22, 2013 5:33 pm Eva Monkey brings up a very valid point, and it's really the only one of my major complaints about the new films. It doesn't bother me so much, as I realize what it takes to make a series of independently produced movies of this scope, but it still nags at me from time to time. A lot of folks bring up the fact that the original NGE TV series and EoE were produced in the span of about 3 years, which is probably how long you'll be waiting for FINAL.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Tue Oct 22, 2013 6:07 pm FreakyFilmFan4ever wrote: Eva Monkey brings up a very valid point, and it's really the only one of my major complaints about the new films. It doesn't bother me so much, as I realize what it takes to make a series of independently produced movies of this scope, but it still nags at me from time to time. A lot of folks bring up the fact that the original NGE TV series and EoE were produced in the span of about 3 years, which is probably how long you'll be waiting for FINAL.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Oct 22, 2013 7:57 pm I'd rather Studio khara take their time than rush production, but it's slightly frustrating how long it's taken for the movies to be churned out. Assuming that khara originally gave Final the 2013 release date was because they've been working on 3.0 and 4.0 back-to-back, I hope Final will hit Japanese theaters by 2015 the latest. Anyway, if you don't mind the wait between each film, check out Rebuild. Funimation's 1.11 and 2.22's blurays on Amazon are very reasonably priced. And according to the rumors, Funimation's 3.33 will be out February 2014.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Tue Oct 22, 2013 8:56 pm Comparing Rebuild's animation quality to NGE is like comparing Miyazaki to South Park. Anyway, I'd say Princess Asuka should wait until the entire series is finished. Fantasizing about where the story should head seems to trip some people up. If you wait days/minutes in between films (as opposed to YEARS) this will be less of a problem. You will also be able to put the entire series in context, which is a constant problem for us.

[]:             Quote Post by KingXanaduu » Wed Oct 23, 2013 12:01 pm Personally, I have seen the movies, and I've only been exposed to certain scenes from 1.11 and 2.22, as well as hundreds of gifs of 3.33 on tumblr. Now, I've promised myself that I wouldn't see the entire movies in DETAIL until Final comes out and I'm at least assure a "somewhat" better ending. Unfortunately, my innate curiosity has forced me to come to Evageeks to reveal most of 3.33, and thus really left a bad impression to me about Rebuild. (Can you blame me? I'm an Optimist ). Thus, because of 3.33, it makes me feel impatient about the release of Final and hard to see the series in context just yet.

[]:             Quote Post by Dataprime » Wed Oct 23, 2013 12:18 pm Sure, if you finished everything else why not. ;) Unless you want to wait until the last film comes out... then you might have to wait awhile

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Oct 23, 2013 12:32 pm The first movie is pretty much a high budget remake of the first half-dozen episodes -- you can see the decade's worth of technological advances in the way that Ramiel was taken from a boring geometric shape to a thing of Lovecraftian geometries rotating in and out of our space. And apart from a few minor things, if you stop after the end of Operation Yashima (that's a few minutes before the end of the movie), you can treat it as a remake, and set it aside at that point. This is roughly what I did -- and having gotten off the Rebuild train after 1.01, I'm waiting to see if there's pay-off enough at the end of the line for it to be worthwhile watching the so far shiny but shallow continuation.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Wed Oct 23, 2013 6:00 pm Yes OP, you should almost certainly watch Rebuild. The films are simply great with an outstanding animation and story which manages to stand very well on it's own, and the waiting time for films (only one movie left, really) isn't a big deal at all. I held off of watching Rebuild for a long time because i pretentiously thought it wouldn't match the original and would be just a soulless, if technically impressive, series of films. I'm really glad to have proven very, very wrong. This might probably be my personal experience projecting, but i would highly recommend you to take *everything* you read about the Rebuild films with a grain of salt, lots of poorly-construed critiques or impressions around, a lot of the content is inherently subjective/divisive, and even accounting for that personal impressions can vary a lot. So yeah, if you're worried about something like Q ending on a bad cliffhanger, i think you should see and decide for yourself.

[]:             Quote Post by Princess Asuka » Wed Oct 23, 2013 6:05 pm I read the manga up to volume 13 and I'm waiting for 14. I also read Angelic Days, but also wanna read the Shinji Ikari raising project.

[]:             Quote Post by ProgButterKnife » Tue Oct 29, 2013 10:23 pm I would I just watched the new stuff in the past 3 weeks, if you keep a open mind of what's to come their quite enjoyable, but I do feel like they are slightly rushed in 2 hour formats which keep characterization limited, and the pace feel like they are trying to hit this and this and that before they move on, however 3.33 avoids this cause of the significant plot changes.

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Tue Oct 29, 2013 10:50 pm Sure, might as well get prepared for the reaction 4.0 produces. It's easy on the eyes, just don't expect anything else from it really.

[]:             Quote Post by Warren Peace » Wed Oct 30, 2013 1:11 am It's quite easy on the eyes, and there's quite a lot to be gotten out of it if you're not a stick in the mud, is what he means

[]:             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Wed Oct 30, 2013 8:02 am Expect about as much of it that 3 hour and a half long movies can bring to the table. Considering its duration, NTE tells a superb story. (You won't find character depth and plot intrigue like that in other 90 minute films, that's for sure.)

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Oct 30, 2013 5:52 pm It all depends on how long you want to wait in between finishing 3.33 and watching the final movie. If you're cool with the wait, watch it as soon as possible; if not, just wait until the fourth movie comes out. You could also do what Evamonkey said and read the manga while you wait. It's really quite good.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Wed Oct 30, 2013 6:19 pm There's no reason not to watch the story so far; after all the films have been released for watching , not for putting on a shelf.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Wed Oct 30, 2013 6:26 pm The only benefit to not watching would be if you wanted to take all of the movies in at once, instead of waiting in between Evangelion dosages.
summary: []: Should I watch Rebuild yet?             Quote Post by Princess Asuka » Tue Oct 22, 2013 4:37 pm I'm debating on whether or not to watch it yet after already having seen the series and End of Evangelion, also local libraries in my area have copies of 1.0, 1.11, and 2.22. I've also heard that 3.33 leaves off on a bad cliffhanger and that I should wait until final comes out before watching rebuild. Any opinions on this are welcome
